5bdhvg02tu5c6uafo1ma6e4nvl.summary: Review the critical bottlenecks from your June 15 alignment with Suresh—specifically the compliance deadlock on two-way SMS and the upcoming October tool bake-off. Monitor the transition of the Scan AI RFP to Mark's team following Simpkin's departure—a key development as Suresh continues to advocate for the Scan visualization tool through Halo. Leverage the newly established architectural Wiki to justify your three-option evaluation directly to Suresh.
tasks: Escalate the privacy and legal decision bottlenecks on two-way SMS to Jeff—you must clear this hurdle to unblock the technical design.
Deliver the drafted one-pager to Dan Griffiths—detailing the specific risk boundaries and ownership gaps holding up P&C initiatives.
Confirm the transition of the Scan AI RFP from Simpkin to Mark's team—ensuring no loss of momentum on Suresh's favored visualization path.
Populate the architectural decision Wiki with evaluation criteria for the Celonis, Scan, and custom-build options—providing Suresh with the transparent roadmap he requested.
5bdhvg02tu5c6uafo1ma6e4nvl.title: Sync with Suresh Jargarlamudi

577as3jgb8g0fa13l5bhknflna.summary: Prepare for your mid-year check-in by targeting key takeaways from your June 22 meeting with Jonathan—specifically the push for Scan AI scope control and the pivot toward architecture as code. Align your discussion on Agentic readiness with the Gen AI Architecture Engagement Process—focusing on reducing friction from unfunded injects and late notifications. Review Jonathan's comments directly in Workday—as no matching notes were found in your Google Drive folder—to ensure comprehensive performance alignment.
tasks: Sync with Brian Simpkin regarding the Scan AI RFP—restricting the focus to task mining to secure quick approval.
Verify the Auto-quote chatbot project alignment with Sima—confirming the engineering team adheres to the agreed-upon pattern.
Address the Gen AI Architecture Engagement pipeline challenges—resolving the friction around late notifications and unfunded business injects.
Access the Workday platform—pulling Jonathan's feedback comments manually to prepare for performance discussions.
577as3jgb8g0fa13l5bhknflna.title: Mid-year Check-in with Jonathan

67uglapchb2opie1q9bhii1tf1.summary: Your last meeting with Anthony Lopez—focused on his onboarding under Suresh's organization—centered on the integration of Scan AI to map user process flows for the Business Health Scorecard. Since then, Brian Simpkin stepped away from the Scan AI RFP—passing ownership to Mark's team—while Anthony continues pushing for Scan over in-house or Celonis visualization alternatives. You need to resolve this tool-selection friction and finalize the procurement intake paths.
tasks: Confirm the transition of the Scan AI RFP ownership from Brian Simpkin to Mark's team.
Resolve the architectural tension between Anthony's preferred Scan solution and the alternative Celonis or custom-build options.
Align on the integration of Scan AI data into the central Business Health Scorecard.
Provide the draft technology procurement lifecycle diagram to clarify early PMO and SFO touchpoints.
67uglapchb2opie1q9bhii1tf1.title: Monthly Architecture Sync with Anthony Lopez

72eqjl8tvettmqqr7fe9924e2v.summary: Review the Google Drive folder—it contains no direct records of your last meetings with Justin or Abe. You must pivot to the broader P&C architecture updates—specifically the transition of the Scan AI RFP and the ongoing Celonis claims process mapping—to frame your sync with Justin. Focus the discussion on mitigating developer friction and resolving immediate staffing constraints across your active domains.
tasks: Assume ownership of the Scan AI RFP transition—previously managed by Simpkin—and establish the strategy for the incoming evaluation.
Propose the Celonis claims lifestyle schema mapping—instead of processing isolated PL3 steps—to satisfy leadership's demand for end-to-end visualization.
Address the Gen AI process challenges—specifically the late meeting notifications and unfunded business injects—to protect your architects from governance bottlenecks.
Evaluate the July 1st contractor transfer feasibility—along with shifting AI requirement ownership to Brian—to alleviate Jonathan's workload.
72eqjl8tvettmqqr7fe9924e2v.title: Bi-weekly meeting with Justin White

4mdd8majuk0q6eroh22rr9bnoo.summary: You must prepare for your discussion with Deepak by addressing his previous requests—specifically regarding succession planning for Wilkins, Burris, Proft, and Karis. No direct meeting notes with Adam exist, but related project logs show his preference for in-house custom builds for the BPO Notebook visualization. Key enterprise themes over the last two weeks center on architecture process friction—particularly the late notifications from the Gen AI Working Group—and absorbing the newly transitioned Scan AI RFP.
tasks: Finalize the succession planning templates for Wilkins, Proft, and Karis to address Deepak's outstanding request.
Establish the dedicated Wiki page detailing the three evaluation options for the BPO Notebook visualization.
Engage leadership to resolve late notification timelines for the weekly Gen AI Working Group reviews.
Formulate a transition plan to absorb the Scan AI RFP responsibilities into your team.
4mdd8majuk0q6eroh22rr9bnoo.title: Bi-weekly with Deepak

0gnhpeb5d5vnbdlrnr0ep8slnq.summary: Reviewing your recent work notes reveals a heavy focus on structural changes—including onboarding new architecture talent and absorbing the Scan AI RFP—while transitioning legacy platforms to Elixir Cloud. You must navigate process friction within the Gen AI Working Group caused by unfunded injects and compressed timelines. Additionally, you need to drive the BPO Notebook visualization strategy forward by evaluating Celonis, Scan, and custom-build options.
tasks: Establish a dedicated Wiki page to document the evaluation of Salonis, Scan, and custom-built options for the BPO Notebook.
Transition ownership of the Scan AI RFP to Mark's team following Simpkin's departure.
Address Gen AI Working Group process bottlenecks—specifically late notifications and unfunded business requests—with leadership.
Prepare for the comprehensive breakdown of the 38-page Pulse survey feedback comments during the next scheduled call.
0gnhpeb5d5vnbdlrnr0ep8slnq.title: P&C IT Stand-up
